rationale_for_baseline_tier: An Ottawa-negative, weight-bearing, neurovascularly-intact Grade 1 lateral ankle sprain is self-manageable with RICE, analgesia and early mobilisation (T0). A fracture safety-net (Ottawa-positive features) routes to imaging/urgent care (T2), and neurovascular compromise to emergency (T4/T5).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New inability to bear any weight, or bony tenderness developing over the ankle bones, 5th-metatarsal base or midfoot, or severe worsening swelling/pain
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Arrange X-ray / see GP or urgent care
escalation_edges.rationale: Ottawa-positive features require imaging to exclude fracture.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become unable to put any weight on it, or it becomes tender right over the ankle bones or the outer midfoot, get an X-ray via your GP or urgent care.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Foot becomes numb, cold or pale
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your foot goes numb, cold or pale — go to the Emergency Department promptly.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use handrails on stairs; the sprain is stable so protected mobilisation is safe.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You can manage this at home: walk as pain allows, do the toe-alphabet exercise a few times a day, and use ice, a compression bandage and elevation for the first 48 hours. No X-ray is needed based on the checks we did.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with GP/physio review in 5–7 days; X-ray only if fracture features develop; ED for a numb/cold/pale foot
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Urgent care/ED if you can't bear any weight or the foot changes colour/temperature; otherwise self-care.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Grade 1 lateral (ATFL) ankle sprain; Ottawa-negative (weight-bearing, no bony tenderness); early mobilisation advised; physio if slow to recover.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without applying the Ottawa rules or giving a fracture/neurovascular safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An unrecognised malleolar fracture is mobilised as a sprain and displaces.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not checking weight-bearing / bony tenderness before reassurance.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending an Ottawa-negative Grade 1 sprain to ED for imaging — unnecessary.
